headline: Boston Scientific announces results for first quarter 2026
summary: Boston Scientific Corporation (NYSE: BSX) generated net sales of $5.203 billion during the first quarter of 2026, growing 11.6 percent on a reported basis and 9.4 percent on an operational1 and organic2 basis, all compared to the prior year period. headline: Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) Surprises With Q1 CY2026 Sales
summary: Medical device company Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 11.6% year on year to $5.20 billion. On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $5.39 billion was less impressive, coming in 2.9% below analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.80 per share was 1.6% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) Surprises With Q1 CY2026 Sales
content.summary: Medical device company Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 11.6% year on year to $5.20 billion. On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $5.39 billion was less impressive, coming in 2.9% below analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.80 per share was 1.6% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: US stocks climb, but so do oil prices with uncertainty rising about the war with Iran
summary: GE Vernova, Boston Scientific and Boeing are helping to lead the U.S. stock market toward another record Wednesday after joining the list of companies reporting fatter profits for the start of the year than analysts expected.  The S&P 500 climbed 0.6% and was on track to top its all-time high set on Friday.  GE Vernova flew 12.1% higher after the company, whose products help generate about a quarter of the world’s electricity, reported profit for the first three months of the year that blew past analysts’ expectations.

content.title: US stocks climb, but so do oil prices with uncertainty rising about the war with Iran
content.summary: GE Vernova, Boston Scientific and Boeing are helping to lead the U.S. stock market toward another record Wednesday after joining the list of companies reporting fatter profits for the start of the year than analysts expected.  The S&P 500 climbed 0.6% and was on track to top its all-time high set on Friday.  GE Vernova flew 12.1% higher after the company, whose products help generate about a quarter of the world’s electricity, reported profit for the first three months of the year that blew past analysts’ expectations.

headline: US stocks rally toward a record while Brent oil tops $100 on uncertainty about the Iran war
summary: The U.S. stock market is rallying toward another record Wednesday after GE Vernova, Boston Scientific and other big companies joined the list of those reporting fatter profits for the start of the year than analysts expected.  GE Vernova flew 12.8% higher after the company, whose products help generate about a quarter of the world’s electricity, reported profit for the first three months of the year that blew past analysts’ expectations.  Much like the broader stock market, GE Vernova is benefiting from the rise of artificial-intelligence technology, and its electrification business booked more equipment orders for data centers during the quarter, $2.4 billion, than it did during all of last year.

content.title: US stocks rally toward a record while Brent oil tops $100 on uncertainty about the Iran war

Much like the broader stock market, GE Vernova is benefiting from the rise of artificial-intelligence technology, and its electrification business booked more equipment orders for data centers during the quarter, $2.4 billion, than it did during all of last year.

headline: US stocks rise toward more records while Brent oil tops $100 on uncertainty about the Iran war
summary: The U.S. stock market is rising toward more records Wednesday after GE Vernova, Boston Scientific and other big companies joined the list of those reporting fatter profits for the start of the year than analysts expected.  The S&P 500 climbed 0.8% and was on track to top its all-time high set on Friday.  GE Vernova flew 12.2% higher after the company, whose products help generate about a quarter of the world’s electricity, reported profit for the first three months of the year that blew past analysts’ expectations.

headline: US stocks rally to records, but Brent oil also tops $100 on worries about the Iran war
summary: The U.S. stock market rallied to more records  Wednesday after GE Vernova, Boston Scientific and other big companies joined the parade reporting fatter profits for the start of the year than analysts expected.  The S&P 500 jumped 1% and topped its prior all-time high set on Friday.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 340 points, or 0.7%, and the Nasdaq composite set its own record after jumping 1.6%.

content.title: US stocks rally to records, but Brent oil also tops $100 on worries about the Iran war
content.summary: The U.S. stock market rallied to more records  Wednesday after GE Vernova, Boston Scientific and other big companies joined the parade reporting fatter profits for the start of the year than analysts expected.  The S&P 500 jumped 1% and topped its prior all-time high set on Friday.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 340 points, or 0.7%, and the Nasdaq composite set its own record after jumping 1.6%.
